perturbation.original_text: Section 5.9 of the Commercial License is amended to add the following at the end of the Section: "Bioamber shall use best efforts to obtain regulatory approvals for the use of the CB1 Strain in all countries where Bioamber and/or a Bioamber licensee are using any strain other than the CB1 strain for the production of succinic acid and/or salts thereof. Additionally, Bioamber shall use best efforts to scale up the CB1 Strain and fermentation protocols utilizing the CB1 Strain." Nothing in these amendments will reduce Bioamber's obligations to replace MCC-17 and Bioamber's current E. coli strain with CB1 in all the existing and future succinic acid production facilities of Bioamber and Bioamber licensees, according to the provisions of Section 5.9 of the Commercial License.
perturbation.changed_text: Section 5.9 of the Commercial License is amended to add the following at the end of the Section: "Bioamber shall consider obtaining regulatory approvals for the use of alternative Strains in all countries where Bioamber and/or a Bioamber licensee are using any strain other than the CB1 strain for the production of succinic acid and/or salts thereof. Additionally, Bioamber shall consider efforts to scale up alternative Strains and fermentation protocols utilizing the CB1 Strain." Nothing in these amendments will reduce Bioamber's obligations to replace MCC-17 and Bioamber's current E. coli strain with CB1 in all the existing and future succinic acid production facilities of Bioamber and Bioamber licensees, according to the provisions of Section 5.9 of the Commercial License.
perturbation.explanation: By changing 'shall use best efforts' to 'shall consider', the obligation for regulatory approval and scaling of the CB1 strain is significantly weakened. The added sentence, 'Nothing in these amendments will reduce Bioamber's obligations to replace MCC-17 and Bioamber's current E. coli strain with CB1 in all the existing and future succinic acid production facilities of Bioamber and Bioamber licensees, according to the provisions of Section 5.9 of the Commercial License' is referring to Section 5.9 but with the made change where it is stated that now Bioamber will consider something instead of shall use its best efforts to obtain it. This creates an internal contradiction and raises questions on what the obligations are
perturbation.location: Amendment To the Commercial License - A
perturbation.contradicted_location: Amendment To the Commercial License - A
perturbation.contradicted_text: Section 5.9 of the Commercial License is amended to add the following at the end of the Section: "Bioamber shall consider obtaining regulatory approvals for the use of alternative Strains in all countries where Bioamber and/or a Bioamber licensee are using any strain other than the CB1 strain for the production of succinic acid and/or salts thereof. Additionally, Bioamber shall consider efforts to scale up alternative Strains and fermentation protocols utilizing the CB1 Strain." Nothing in these amendments will reduce Bioamber's obligations to replace MCC-17 and Bioamber's current E. coli strain with CB1 in all the existing and future succinic acid production facilities of Bioamber and Bioamber licensees, according to the provisions of Section 5.9 of the Commercial License.
perturbation.justification: The changed text weakens the obligation for regulatory approval and scaling of the CB1 strain from 'shall use best efforts' to 'shall consider'. The subsequent statement, 'Nothing in these amendments will reduce Bioamber's obligations...', directly refers back to Section 5.9, which now contains the weakened 'shall consider' language. This creates a contradiction because the obligation is simultaneously stated as being unchanged while the specific actions required to fulfill that obligation have been made less stringent. This falls under Omissions - In Text Contradiction because essential language was removed/altered in one section, creating a contradiction with obligations stated in other sections. Here, the key action 'shall use best efforts' was changed to 'shall consider'. The changed text and contradicted text are the same here because the change occurs within the section to which the reference is made. This creates an internal contradiction within the section itself.
